43: **Argentina** and **Austria** are both level on 3 points in **Group J** — each with a win from their opener but positions not yet separated — making this a match with genuine top-of-the-group stakes. **Argentina**'s superior goal difference gives them a slender advantage on paper, but **Austria** arrive in form and full of confidence, and this is exactly the kind of structured, high-intensity European side that can make life difficult for even the most decorated opposition.
42: **France**, level on 3 points in **Group I** alongside **Norway** with positions not yet separated, take on **Iraq**, who are also level with **Senegal** on zero points with the bottom two spots equally unresolved. **France** have the quality across every line to dominate this fixture, but **Iraq** need a result desperately and will defend deep and look to catch Les Bleus on the break — making patience and precision the key for Deschamps' side.
41: **Norway**, level on 3 points in **Group I** with **France** and positions not yet separated, face a **Senegal** side also level with **Iraq** on zero points, their standing at the bottom equally unresolved. This is a must-not-lose for **Senegal**, whose tournament survival depends on finding a response, but **Norway** have looked clinical in the final third and will back themselves to push for the group lead with another assertive performance.
44: **Jordan** and **Algeria** close out Matchday 2 in **Group J** with both sides level on zero points — each sharing the bottom half of the group with positions not yet separated — in a match that is effectively an elimination decider. Whichever side finds the drive and composure to take three points keeps their knockout hopes alive; the other faces a near-impossible final group game to avoid going home early.
38: **Spain** and **Saudi Arabia** meet in **Group H** with both sides level on a single point among all four teams — positions entirely unseparated — meaning neither can afford a second straight draw. **Spain** bring the pressure of expectation and superior individual quality, but **Saudi Arabia** have already shown resilience this stage and will look to frustrate and hit on the counter in what could be a genuinely tight affair.
39: **Belgium** and **IR Iran** are both level on one point in a completely deadlocked **Group G**, where all four sides share identical records and no position has yet been separated. The Red Devils have the attacking firepower to break the stalemate, but **IR Iran**'s disciplined defensive block made life uncomfortable in their opener, and they'll back themselves to nick something here if **Belgium** grow impatient.
37: **Uruguay** and **Cabo Verde** both sit level on one point in **Group H** alongside the group's other two sides, with positions still entirely unresolved heading into this 3:00 PM PT showdown. **Uruguay**'s battle-hardened South American pedigree makes them slight favourites, but **Cabo Verde** have proven they are no pushovers and a point here could still be enough to keep their knockout hopes very much alive.
40: **New Zealand** and **Egypt** close out a pivotal day for **Group G** at 6:00 PM PT, both sitting level on a single point in a group where all four teams remain completely inseparable. With **Belgium** and **IR Iran** having kicked off earlier, one or both of these sides may know exactly what they need — and the All Whites and the Pharaohs will leave everything on the pitch to avoid falling off the pace.
